reasoning: Neither `container_perms_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` constrains `owned_threads`. The preconditions are simultaneously satisfiable, confirming two distinct containers can own the same thread, violating unique ownership intent from code comments.

`new_container_ensures` constrains children, subtree_set, uppertree_seq, depth, and parent but never requires `owned_threads@ =~= Set::empty()`. A freshly created container should logically start with no threads.

Same pattern as owned_threads — `new_container_ensures` never requires `owned_endpoints@ =~= Set::empty()`. A freshly created container should have no endpoints, yet the spec permits arbitrary endpoint sets.
The spec never relates `scheduler` contents to `owned_threads`. A container can schedule threads it doesn't own, breaking the containment model where only owned threads should be schedulable.

`new_container_ensures` never constrains the new container's quota relative to its parent. However, quota allocation may intentionally be handled at a different layer (the commented-out `mem_quota`/`mem_used` fields suggest quota logic is still in development), so this may be an intentional omission rather than a spec gap.
